calendar.matters:
Petitioner'S Ex Parte Application For Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE INJUNCTION

Appearances: Matthew H. Burrows and Victoria R. Nuetzel of San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit (510.464.6023) appearing for Respondent San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit; Jonathan H. Siegel of Siegel Lewitter Malkani (510.452.5000) for Respondent American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees, Local 3993; Kerianne R. Steele of Weinberg Roger & Rosenfeld (510.337.1001) appearing for Respondent Service Employees International Union, Local 1021; Sharon L. O'Grady and Tamar Pachter of the Office of the Attorney General (415.703.5899) appearing for Petitioner and moving party; Peter Saltzman of Leonard Carder LLP (415.771.6400) appearing for Respondent Amalgamated Transit Union, Local 1555

This matter is specially set for emergency hearing on Sunday, August 11, 2013, at 9:00 am in Department 304. Ms. O'Grady and Mr. Saltzman present preliminary statements to the Court.

The Court states its findings as follows: 1) it has jurisdiction, 2) there are no procedural problems with the request, 3) Government Code 3614 controls, and 4) the parties agree that the conditions are true.

There being no objections to the findings or the request for Preliminary Injunction, an Order to Show Cause will not issue and the Preliminary Injunction is granted. Counsel is directed to modify the proposed form of order and email it to the Court for signature. The Court will set a Case Management Conference after October 10, 2013 in the event the matter is not resolved. Counsel stipulate that service of the order may be completed by email. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Rhonda L. Aquilina, CSR 956
